Hybrid temporal signature clustering for multi-series time-course proteomics

Source: R/tools.R
temporalSignatureClustering.Rd

Cluster proteins by coordinated temporal response patterns across one or multiple biological time series. The method first estimates log2 fold change against the baseline time point within each series, gates non-significant comparisons to zero, and clusters the remaining dynamic proteins with a hybrid distance that combines response shape, amplitude, temporal trend and significant-response timing.

Proteins with no statistically supported post-baseline change are not forced into dynamic clusters. They are returned in the background group Flat_or_not_significant.

Usage

temporalSignatureClustering(
  x,
  assay_name = "conc",
  time_col = "time",
  series_col = NULL,
  baseline = NULL,
  k = "auto",
  k_range = 2:10,
  min_mean = 0,
  min_sd = 0.15,
  log2_offset = NULL,
  scale_for_clustering = FALSE,
  distance = c("correlation", "euclidean"),
  p_adj_cutoff = 0.05,
  logFC_cutoff = 0.5,
  min_cluster_prop = 0.2,
  common_features = TRUE,
  max_k_features = 2000,
  min_significant_timepoints = 1,
  clustering_method = c("hybrid_hclust", "kmeans"),
  distance_weights = c(shape = 0.35, amplitude = 0.25, trend = 0.25, timing = 0.15),
  seed = 1
)

Arguments

x

A SummarizedExperiment object, or a named list of gene-by-time matrices. For a SummarizedExperiment, samples are in columns and features/proteins are in rows.

assay_name

Character. Assay used when x is a SummarizedExperiment. Default is "conc".

time_col

Character. Column in colData(x) containing ordered time points. Default is "time".

series_col

Character or NULL. Column in colData(x) containing the biological series, such as cell line. If NULL, all samples are treated as one series.

baseline

Baseline time point. If NULL, the first ordered time point in each series is used.

k

Integer number of clusters or "auto". When "auto", the cluster number is selected by average silhouette width.

k_range

Integer vector of candidate cluster numbers used when k = "auto".

min_mean

Numeric. Minimum mean raw abundance for feature filtering.

min_sd

Numeric. Minimum standard deviation of the baseline-referenced trajectory for feature filtering.

log2_offset

Numeric or NULL. Offset added before log2 transformation. If NULL, half of the minimum positive abundance is used.

scale_for_clustering

Logical. If TRUE, scale the significance-gated matrix before clustering. Default is FALSE.

distance

Character. Distance used by the optional kmeans workflow; one of "correlation" or "euclidean".

p_adj_cutoff

Numeric adjusted P-value cutoff used to define significant time points.

logFC_cutoff

Numeric absolute log2FC cutoff used together with p_adj_cutoff.

min_cluster_prop

Numeric. Cluster-level fraction threshold used to call a cluster response time.

common_features

Logical. If TRUE, retain only features observed in all input series.

max_k_features

Integer. Maximum number of proteins used to estimate the silhouette score when selecting k. The final clustering still uses all retained dynamic proteins.

min_significant_timepoints

Integer. Minimum number of significant post-baseline time points required for a protein to enter dynamic clustering. Default is 1.

clustering_method

Character. Clustering backend. Default "hybrid_hclust" uses the hybrid temporal distance; "kmeans" is retained for compatibility.

distance_weights

Named numeric vector with entries shape, amplitude, trend and timing. Values are normalized to sum to one.

seed

Integer random seed.

Value

A list with class EasyProteinTimecoursePattern. Important elements include:

gene_cluster

Dynamic proteins and their assigned pattern clusters.

gene_cluster_all

All proteins, including background proteins labeled Flat_or_not_significant.

background_genes

Proteins without enough significant time-point changes for dynamic clustering.

gene_change

Gene-level first significant and strongest response times.

cluster_change

Cluster-level significant response times.

cluster_summary

Cluster sizes, centroid pattern labels and activity summaries.

cluster_input

The significance-gated log2FC matrix used for clustering.

timepoint_tests

limma time-point test results.

Details

For each protein \(g\), series \(s\), and time point \(t\), the method estimates a baseline-referenced log2 fold change \(L_{g,s,t}\). The clustering matrix is

$$ Z_{g,s,t} = \begin{cases} L_{g,s,t}, & q_{g,s,t}\leq \alpha \text{ and } |L_{g,s,t}|\geq \delta,\\ 0, & \text{otherwise}. \end{cases} $$

Dynamic proteins are clustered using a hybrid distance:

$$ D = w_{shape}D_{shape} + w_{amp}D_{amp} + w_{trend}D_{trend} + w_{time}D_{time}. $$

This makes the clustering sensitive to both response magnitude and temporal trajectory, while treating non-significant comparisons as explicit no-change observations.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
res <- temporalSignatureClustering(
  se,
  assay_name = "conc",
  time_col = "time",
  series_col = "cell_line",
  baseline = 0,
  k = "auto",
  k_range = 4:10
)

head(res$gene_cluster)
res$cluster_summary
} # }
